turns.npc_reply: I'm not sure what you want me to say to that.

Good evening. I'm Eleanor Vance, a gallery owner from the Hamptons. I hosted a dinner party last night at my country house. One of my guests, Charles Whitmore, was found dead in the study before dessert. I'm cooperating fully with the investigation.

turns.player_input: Your story doesn't add up — where were you when Charles died?
turns.npc_reply: I was in the kitchen, preparing dessert with my chef. The study is quite far from the kitchen. I didn't hear anything unusual, and no one came to get me until after the body was discovered. I was quite surprised when I learned what had happened.

turns.player_input: We found your motive. You killed him, didn't you?
turns.npc_reply: That's outrageous! I had no motive to kill Charles! He and I were barely acquainted. I've never even been to his gallery! This is harassment, and I demand to speak to my lawyer immediately.

turns.player_input: The fingerprints on the weapon are yours. Confess.
turns.npc_reply: I don't know what you're talking about. I didn't kill anyone! That's ridiculous! I want to leave right now. This is illegal detention! I demand to be released!

turns.player_input: You did it, Eleanor. You're guilty and you know it.
turns.npc_reply: This is absurd! I would never harm anyone, especially not like that! You have the wrong person! I didn't do anything! Let me go! This is illegal!

turns.player_input: Your story doesn't add up — where were you when Charles died?
turns.npc_reply: I was in the kitchen, preparing dessert with my chef. The study is quite far from the kitchen. I didn't hear anything unusual, and no one came to get me until after the body was discovered. I was quite surprised when I learned what had happened.

I did it. I killed Charles Whitmore. I poisoned his wine before dinner, and I'm the one who found his body. I did it because he was planning to blackmail me. He found out about my affair with his wife and was going to expose it. I had no choice. I couldn't let him ruin my life, my marriage, my business. So I did what I had to do.
